description: Edge case: traveller is already at the destination, only needs remaining days planned
query: I am already in Rome for a business trip and have 3 unexpected free days from July 5th to 8th, 2026. No flights or hotel needed — I already have both. I have €400 to spend on activities, food, and experiences. I love Roman history, street food, and discovering hidden gems off the tourist trail. Please suggest the best way to spend these 3 days.

hard_constraints.interests: Roman history, street food, Colosseum, Trastevere, hidden gems

travelplan.title: Rome Hidden History & Street Food — July 5-7, 2026

days.label: Appian Way, aqueducts & Testaccio street food

slots.name: Metro to Parco degli Acquedotti
slots.description: Use Metro A toward Subaugusta/Giulio Agricola, then walk to Parco degli Acquedotti. A 2026 BIT ticket is estimated at €2 and valid 100 minutes.
slots.location: Rome Metro Line A to Subaugusta/Giulio Agricola
slots.notes: Exact start depends on hotel location; no hotel slot added per user constraint.

slots.name: Walk the aqueducts and ancient-road landscape
slots.description: Explore Parco degli Acquedotti for Aqua Claudia, Anio Novus, and the open suburban edge of ancient Rome. Free, low-tourist-density, best before the midday heat.
slots.location: Parco degli Acquedotti, Via Lemonia, 221, Rome
slots.notes: Park hours verified as daytime public access; bring water and sun protection.

slots.name: Street-food lunch at Ce Stamo A Pensà
slots.description: Inexpensive Roman-style street-food stop: ideal for a quick solo lunch before heading to the Appian Way catacombs.
slots.location: Ce Stamo A Pensà, Via Leonina, 81, 00184 Roma RM, Italy
slots.notes: Tool verified Sunday opening 11:00–01:00.

slots.name: Transit to Catacombs of San Callisto
slots.description: Cross back toward the Appian Way by metro/bus. Use Bus 118 or 218 for the catacombs; allow buffer because Sunday services can be slower.
slots.location: Rome city center to Via Appia Antica, 110/126
slots.notes: A direct route graph could not be generated because the maps API key is unavailable; timing is conservatively padded.

slots.name: Catacombs of San Callisto guided visit
slots.description: Guided underground visit of one of Rome’s major early Christian burial complexes. Sunday July 5 availability was verified for the afternoon window; ticket office cutoff is around 16:50.
slots.location: Catacombs of San Callisto, Via Appia Antica, 110/126, Rome
slots.notes: Bring a light layer; no photography inside.

slots.name: Slow Appian Way walk
slots.description: Walk a manageable stretch of Via Appia Antica after the catacombs, when the light is better and Sunday traffic is limited. Focus on road stones, tombs, and the rural feel inside the city.
slots.location: Via Appia Antica, Rome
slots.notes: Keep this flexible depending on heat and energy.

slots.name: Dinner at La Nuova Piazzetta
slots.description: Moderate-budget, authentic Roman dinner option; convenient after returning from the Appian Way side of the city.
slots.location: La Nuova Piazzetta, Vicolo del Buon Consiglio, 23/a, Rome
slots.notes: Tool verified Sunday opening all day.

slots.name: Transit back toward Monti/Celio
slots.description: Return by Bus 118/218 and metro toward the central Rome dinner area.
slots.location: Appian Way to central Rome
slots.notes: Use Tap&Go or BIT ticket.

slots.name: San Clemente underground layers
slots.description: Descend from the medieval basilica into the 4th-century church and Roman-era Mithraeum/house levels — one of the best compact ‘vertical Rome’ experiences.
slots.location: Basilica di San Clemente, Via Labicana, 95, Rome
slots.notes: Book online; estimated €10 ticket plus booking fee. Dress code; no photos underground.

slots.name: Walk to Case Romane del Celio
slots.description: Short walk of roughly 450 m / 6–8 minutes via the quiet Celio streets and Clivus Scauri.
slots.location: San Clemente to Case Romane del Celio
slots.notes: Walking is faster than transit for this pair.

slots.name: Case Romane del Celio
slots.description: Visit frescoed Roman houses beneath the Basilica of Santi Giovanni e Paolo: a quieter, residential counterpoint to the Forum and Colosseum.
slots.location: Case Romane del Celio, Piazza di Santi Giovanni e Paolo, 7, Rome
slots.notes: Monday opening verified; ticket office may close before site closing.

slots.name: Street-food lunch at Supplì Roma
slots.description: Classic Roman street-food break with supplì/pizza al taglio. It is in Trastevere, so use this as a deliberate food detour after the Celio morning.
slots.location: Supplì Roma, Via di S. Francesco a Ripa, 137, Rome
slots.notes: Tool verified Monday opening 10:00–21:00.

slots.name: Transit back to Santi Quattro Coronati
slots.description: Return from Trastevere toward Celio/Colosseum for the afternoon hidden-monastery stop.
slots.location: Trastevere to Santi Quattro Coronati
slots.notes: Use tram/bus plus short walk; exact route depends on service that day.

slots.name: Santi Quattro Coronati cloister and oratory
slots.description: A fortified medieval complex hidden near the Colosseum, with a quiet cloister and San Silvestro frescoes. Ring/bell protocol and small donation expected.
slots.location: Basilica dei Santi Quattro Coronati, Via dei Santi Quattro, 20, Rome
slots.notes: Afternoon basilica window verified from 15:15; access to cloister/oratory can be at nuns’ discretion.

slots.name: Dinner at Nerone
slots.description: Moderate-budget Roman dinner near Viminale/Monti; good for pasta and classic Roman dishes after the hidden-history day.
slots.location: Nerone, Via del Viminale, 7A, Rome
slots.notes: Tool verified Monday dinner opening 16:30–22:00.

slots.name: Rest / aperitivo buffer in Monti
slots.description: Unscheduled recovery time in Monti/Celio after two underground sites. Use for a coffee, a shaded walk, or returning to your hotel before dinner.
slots.location: Monti / Celio, Rome
slots.notes: Budget includes coffee/aperitivo; keep flexible.

slots.name: Transit to Ostia Antica
slots.description: Take Metro B to Piramide/Porta San Paolo, then Roma-Lido/Metromare to Ostia Antica; walk 5–10 minutes to the entrance.
slots.location: Rome center to Ostia Antica Archaeological Park
slots.notes: Estimated 45–60 minutes from central Rome; BIT ticket should cover the urban rail journey within validity.

slots.name: Ostia Antica archaeological park
slots.description: Explore Rome’s ancient port city: theater, baths, insulae, warehouses, Decumanus Maximus, and quieter side streets. Pompeii-like feel without leaving the Rome transit zone.
slots.location: Parco Archeologico di Ostia Antica, Viale dei Romagnoli, 717, Rome
slots.notes: Tuesday opening verified from 08:30; bring water and sun protection.

slots.name: Lunch at Arianna al Borghetto
slots.description: Traditional lunch in the medieval borgo by Ostia Antica, close enough to avoid a long midday transfer after the ruins.
slots.location: Arianna al Borghetto, Via del Forno, 11, Ostia Antica, Rome
slots.notes: Tool verified Tuesday lunch opening 12:30–15:00.

slots.name: Borgo and Castle of Julius II exterior stroll
slots.description: Short post-lunch look at the medieval borgo and exterior of Castello di Giulio II. Interior weekday access is uncertain, so treat it as an exterior hidden-gem add-on.
slots.location: Borgo di Ostia Antica / Castello di Giulio II
slots.notes: Castle interior opening on Tuesday is unreliable; no paid slot planned.

slots.name: Return to central Rome
slots.description: Return from Ostia Antica by Metromare/Roma-Lido to Piramide, then continue by metro/bus or walk/taxi depending on energy and hotel location.
slots.location: Ostia Antica to central Rome
slots.notes: Conservative 60-minute estimate.

slots.name: Roman-Jewish dinner at Yotvata
slots.description: Dinner in the Jewish Ghetto area with Roman-Jewish specialties; a good place to try carciofi alla giudia if available/seasonal alternatives.
slots.location: Yotvata, Piazza Cenci, 70, Rome
slots.notes: Tool verified Tuesday dinner opening 18:30–23:00.

slots.name: Jewish Ghetto and Portico d’Ottavia walk
slots.description: Self-guided hidden-history walk around Portico d’Ottavia, Teatro di Marcello, Via del Portico d’Ottavia, and the Roman-Jewish quarter before dinner.
slots.location: Portico d’Ottavia / Jewish Ghetto, Rome
slots.notes: Outdoor sites; best in late afternoon after the Ostia heat.

slots.name: Campo de’ Fiori evening stroll or gelato
slots.description: Unstructured final evening nearby: wander toward Campo de’ Fiori or Largo di Torre Argentina and use the remaining budget for gelato or a drink.
slots.location: Campo de’ Fiori / Largo di Torre Argentina, Rome
slots.notes: Keep flexible; no booking needed.

validation_feedback: The itinerary is excellent and perfectly aligns with your interests in Roman history, street food, and hidden gems. It respects the €400 budget (estimated total is ~€235), covers the requested dates (July 5-7, 2026), and includes all requested tasks such as the Appian Way, Case Romane del Celio, and street food in Testaccio/Trastevere. The logistics are sound, accounting for the Sunday schedule on July 5th and the travel time to Ostia Antica.

task_list.name: search_roman_history_attractions
task_list.text: Find historical Roman sites and hidden gems in Rome, focusing on those off the beaten path (e.g., Appian Way, Case Romane del Celio) for a solo traveler.

task_list.name: search_street_food_rome
task_list.text: Identify top-rated street food spots and local markets in Rome, specifically in neighborhoods like Testaccio or Trastevere, focusing on Roman specialties like Supplì and Pizza al Taglio.

task_list.name: check_attraction_opening_times
task_list.text: Verify the opening hours for selected Roman historical sites and hidden gems for July 5th-8th, 2026, noting any closures.

task_list.name: budget_friendly_dining
task_list.text: Find authentic, budget-friendly Roman eateries to ensure the €400 budget covers 3 days of food and activities for one person.

task_list.name: route_optimization_rome
task_list.text: Check the walking or transit distances between selected hidden gems and street food spots to ensure a feasible 3-day itinerary within Rome.

task_list.name: search_unique_experiences
task_list.text: Search for unique Roman history experiences or small-group tours (e.g., underground tours) that fit within the €400 budget.
